novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal omits Sigstad2024, 'Marginal Treatment Effects and Monotonicity', a direct MTE monotonicity-robustness comparator that should be separated from the fixed-b derivative-feasibility kernel.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem states exact measurable intensities for every r(P) in K_b^proj, but K_b^proj is defined as a bounded-Lipschitz closure of projections; closure membership need not attain an H_b representation unless closedness/attainment is assumed or the theorem is restricted to Pi(H_b).

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Theorem 1 by distinguishing actual feasible model/Pi(H_b) membership from closure membership, or add an explicit compactness/closed-projection assumption that guarantees attainment.
Add Sigstad2024 as a related-work comparator and explain why weaker monotonicity for MTE identification does not subsume the fixed-envelope derivative feasibility certificate.
Add a finite-grid lemma that spells out the Farkas certificate before the BL limit; this would make the finite-threshold support-certificate part of Conjecture 1 less opaque.

recommended_next_step: Revise the closure/attainment wording in Theorem 1 and add the missing MTE-monotonicity robustness comparator before re-review.
